purpose: Realistic Trellis-flavored trigger prompts for the 8 first-wave Hormozi skills. Use with skill-creator's run_loop.py / run_eval.py to score description triggering accuracy. Each skill has should-trigger (positive) and should-not-trigger (near-miss negatives that pull toward sibling skills or generic plugin skills).
notes: Negatives are deliberately tricky — they share keywords with the target skill but actually belong to a sibling. A skill that triggers on these is leaking scope.

should_trigger: we're rebuilding the DR youth-camp offer for the spring season — current registration page is converting at like 1.8% on cold paid traffic, can you walk me through a Grand Slam audit and tell me what's missing on dream outcome and risk reversal
I want to add a guarantee to the OEV booking offer. Right now we promise 'satisfaction guaranteed' which means nothing. Help me engineer something with teeth that won't tank margin
the CTS catering quote page just describes the package and the price. it has no bonus stack, no naming, nothing irresistible. rebuild this offer for me end-to-end
audit this offer text against the value equation and tell me which of the four levers is weakest: dream outcome, perceived likelihood, time delay, or effort/sacrifice
should_not_trigger: we need to design the full first-30-days journey for new DR families — attraction offer, upsell to franchise, downsell, continuity. what should the offer sequence look like (→ /money-models)
I want to raise prices on existing OEV transport contracts that have been frozen at 2023 rates — write me a price-raise rollout plan (→ /price-raise)
we have 800 warm leads on the CTS list and need to generate cash this week with a 7-day promo (→ /fast-cash)

should_trigger: pricing is all over the place across our brands. I don't know if I should be talking philosophy, structure, or running a test. help me figure out which lever to pull first on DR camp pricing
general question on pricing for OEV — not sure where to start, can you route me
I want to do something about pricing this quarter but I'm not sure if it's posture, model, or just tactical tweaks
should_not_trigger: show me Hormozi's commodity-trap argument and why premium pricing is a virtuous cycle (→ /pricing-posture directly, not the router)
I have current paying customers on a $97/month plan and want to move them to $147 — write me the letter (→ /price-raise directly)
what's the right step size for a price test on the DR add-on — 10% or 25%? (→ /price-test directly)

should_trigger: I keep getting talked down on price during DR franchise discovery calls. starting to wonder if we're underpricing the whole thing — what's the case for going premium and holding the line
explain Hormozi's commodity trap to me. I think CTS catering is sliding into it because we keep matching competitor discounts on weddings
is there a real argument for pricing OEV transport above the market or are we just being arrogant — what's the virtuous-cycle logic
should_not_trigger: what specific tactic should I deploy this month to lift margin on existing OEV contracts — like a processing fee passthrough (→ /pricing-plays)
should the CTS pricing tiers be value-based or cost-plus, and how should the packaging look (→ /pricing-model)
we need to roll out a 30% increase to grandfathered DR families — what's the math and the letter (→ /price-raise)

should_trigger: we're launching a new DR franchise tier next quarter and need to set the initial price structure — cost-plus, competitor-based, or value-based? what model fits youth sports licensing
audit the CTS catering pricing page architecture against Hormozi's 13 pricing rules — I think we're violating at least 3 of them
explain the profit-leverage thesis to me with numbers — why does a 1% price change matter so much more than a 1% cost change in our margin profile
should_not_trigger: I want to send a price-raise letter to existing DR families this month — help me draft it (→ /price-raise)
should I add a processing-fee passthrough to OEV bookings to lift margin this week (→ /pricing-plays)
design a 4-week cohort price test for the new CTS package — what step size and sample size (→ /price-test)

should_trigger: give me 3 instant-profit pricing tactics I can deploy on OEV new bookings this week — billing change, fee passthrough, anchor tweak, anything that doesn't require renegotiating with anyone
we want to pass through Stripe processing fees to CTS catering quotes going forward. is that one of Hormozi's plays and how do we frame it on the invoice
what's the 28-day billing trick and would it work on the DR youth-camp monthly recurring tier
annual CPI bump on new contracts only — quick pricing win, walk me through it
should_not_trigger: we need to apply a 15% price lift to ALL current OEV recurring contracts including grandfathered ones — what's the math and the letter (→ /price-raise; existing customers)
should premium pricing posture make sense for our DR franchise tier or are we overreaching (→ /pricing-posture)
design the cohort split and 30-day measurement plan for the price test (→ /price-test)

should_trigger: set up a structured price test for the new CTS premium catering package — what step size from $4500 to $5500, how many quotes do I need, how long do I run it before calling it
I want to A/B-test two prices on the DR camp landing page — $497 vs $597. cadence, sample size, what's the minimum I need before I trust the result
how do I track Price × Conversion × Churn × LTV during a price test for OEV recurring transport
what's the operating loop for figuring out if my new price actually worked or I just got lucky on a small sample
should_not_trigger: we want to raise prices on existing CTS retainer clients and need the letter and rollout (→ /price-raise; existing customers, not a test)
should I be charging premium at all for OEV — is the posture defensible (→ /pricing-posture)
give me the 10 instant-profit pricing tactics from Hormozi (→ /pricing-plays)

should_trigger: we have 60 DR franchise locations on a grandfathered $1500/month rate from 2022 and we need to move them to $2200/month. write the RAISE letter and tell me what notice window to give
OEV has 12 corporate transport contracts that have been at the same rate for 3 years. how do I raise prices on them without losing the top-3 by revenue
draft a price-increase email for existing CTS catering retainer clients — I want grandfather logic for top tenure, lift for everyone else
what math do I need to run before raising prices on existing customers — what churn can the increase absorb
should_not_trigger: what should the initial pricing structure look like for the brand-new DR franchise tier launching in Q3 (→ /pricing-model; new offer, no existing customers)
I want to test whether $597 outperforms $497 on the DR camp page (→ /price-test; new-customer cohort test)
is premium pricing even defensible for OEV — give me the philosophy (→ /pricing-posture)

should_trigger: I'm not sure what Trellis Fields actually stands for as a brand. help me run the Hormozi pairing exercise — who are we, who do we polarize, what do we measurably stand for
audit the DR brand against Reach, Influence, and Direction — I think we have reach but no direction and the bouquet is getting muddled across CTS / OEV / Trellis
should we pivot the OEV brand or extend it — applying Hormozi's brand bouquet logic to the portfolio
what's our level of authority right now in the youth sports space and how do I move from category-known to first-named
should_not_trigger: help me pick the right ICP for the new DR franchise tier — who exactly should we target (→ /avatar)
set up a system to capture testimonials and UGC from CTS catering customers continuously (→ /marketing-machine)
what should we charge for the premium Trellis Fields offering and is the brand strong enough to support it (→ /pricing-posture)
write headline copy for the new CTS catering homepage hero (→ marketing-skills:copywriting, not branding strategy)
